title: Water Service Payment Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Utility Assistance > Water Service Payment Assistance 78 Matching Service Providers ACCESS ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance with electric, gas, heating fuel, and water bills. Assistance is based on available funding and is typically offered once per year per household. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Households at or below 60% of the Area Median Income (AMI) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 779-9020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADVANCED HEALTH FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 883-2947 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY AND CENTRAL COAST ST JOSEPH FAMILY SHELTER MISSION BENEDICT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry. When funding is available, offers emergency financial assistance for electricity, gas, water/sewer, and prescription costs. 840 S Main Street Mount Angel, OR 97362 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday 3pm-6pm, Wednesday 11am-2pm, Thursday-Friday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 385-3231 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF ASHLAND ASHLAND MUNICIPAL UTILITY EMERGENCY HEAT ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides electric, water, and sewer payment assistance. Assistance is limited to once per year and depends on funding available. 2245 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Must have a disconnect notice, household must meet income limits, and utility must not be more then $100 past due Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Office: Tuesday/Friday 9am-1pm Email (541) 488-6004 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF FOREST GROVE UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides electric and water bill payment assistance when funding is available. 1924 Council Street Forest Grove, OR 971160326 Eligibility: Low income households who are current customer of the City of Forest Grove Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 992-3221 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF GRESHAM UTILITY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited emergency funds to assist City of Gresham customers with paying water, sewer, and stormwater bills only. Assistance is limited to once every six billing cycles, and the program will close once funds are exhausted. 1333 NW Eastman Parkway Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Customers of City of Gresham Utilities who live in free standing homes, duplexes, or triplexes. Priority is given towards those making 50% of Area Median Income (AMI). Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 618-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND WATER BUREAU CRISIS VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Eligible households experiencing a financial crisis may receive a voucher for up to $500 in assistance once every 12 months. 664 N Tillamook Street Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Household must be enrolled in the City of Portland Water Discount Program. The customer must pay a portion of the bill in order to receive assistance and must be in a financial crisis, such as impending shutoff or severe financial problems, that make it impossible to work out a payment plan. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 823-7770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF SHERWOOD LOW INCOME/HARDSHIP ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance in the form of a water or sewer bill credit for up to two months, or approximately $115. Credit is available once per year per household. 22560 SW Pine Street Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following guidelines: -Have a utility bill issued by the City of Sherwood (COS) for water, sewer, street, and surface water management services -Have been an active COS utility billing customer at the same address for at least four months -Meet income guidelines for 60% of State Median Income OR -Are undergoing a hardship such as unexpected medical expenses, divorce, a deceased family member, or natural disaster Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm (503) 925-2315 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES UTILITY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electricity, natural gas, oil, propane, wood or pellets, and select water/wastewater bills. Funds are limited, and assistance is not guaranteed. May not be eligible for funding if recently received assistance and have not completed the waiting period to be eligible again. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: All applicants must have a gross monthly income that is at or below 60% of the Oregon State median income for their household size Heating Assistance: October 1-April 15: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, and medically fragile households November 1-April 15: Households with children age 0-6 December 1-April 15: All income-eligible households Cooling Assistance: May 1-September 15: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, and medically fragile households June 1-September 15: Open to the general public Water Assistance: Customers of Sunrise Water Authority Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 650-5640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA PACIFIC CCO FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Utility payment assistance for water, electricity, natural gas, oil, propane, wood, pellets, and other heating fuels. Payments are issued directly to vendors and assistance may only be received once over per 12 month period. 1565 N 1st Street Suite 1 Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: LIHEAP: 60% State Median Income Emergency Assistance: Must have shut-off notice or have been disconnected Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Utility payment assistance for water, electricity, natural gas, oil, propane, wood, pellets, and other heating fuels. Payments are issued directly to vendors and assistance may only be received once over per 12 month period. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: LIHEAP: 60% State Median Income Emergency Assistance: Must have shut-off notice or have been disconnected Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION RESOURCE ENTERPRISES INC EMERGENCY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with utility bills, one-time rent payments, and prescriptions. May have personal items such as clothes, hygiene products, diapers, and pet supplies, depending on donations received. 2310 1st Street Suite 2 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 842-5261 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO MULTI-SERVICE CENTER UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial help with garbage, water, internet, electric, and heating fuel bills (oil, propane, firewood, and pellets). Payments go directly to the utility company. Applications are processed in the order received, with an estimated wait time of 4–6 weeks. Assistance is based on available funds. 1001 SW Baseline Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Household is at or below 60% of the Oregon Area Median Income (AMI). See website for a list of eligible utility companies. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm, closed national holidays Email (503) 615-0771 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC BAKER COUNTY UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, gas, heating fuel, and water payments. 2810 Cedar Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Must meet income limits Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-4pm Email (541) 523-6591 1505 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC GRANT COUNTY UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, gas, heating fuel, and water payments. 530 E Main Street Suite 6 John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Must meet income limits Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 575-2949 1600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC UNION COUNTY UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, gas, heating fuel, and water payments. 1504 Albany Street La Grande, OR 978502621 Eligibility: Must meet income limits Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 963-7532 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC WALLOWA COUNTY UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, gas, heating fuel, and water payments. 702 NW First Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Must meet income limits Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 426-3840 1202 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY IN ACTION UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides electric, gas, heating fuel, and water assistance to low-income households. An energy education program provides information on energy and water use efficiency, usually in a classroom setting through presentations and hands-on learning. Allows energy assistance and other clients access to items and information that can be used in the home to improve their energy and water use efficiency. 915 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Electric, Gas, and Heating payment assistance: Household income at or below 60% of Oregon's median income. First-priority clients are age 60 and older and people with disabilities. Second-priority clients are families with children age 6 and younger in the household. If funding remains, assistance is offered to the general community. Water payment assistance: EOCCO Medicaid Member with full medical benefits, 30% or less than area median income, have a medical need (Mental or physical health as long as treatment is being received.) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 889-9555 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance for utility bills. Assistance depends on available funding. The waitlist for LIHEAP opens November 1 and is sometimes closed by the following day. All other emergency assistance programs are utility company-specific. Waitlists for funds are established when the funding becomes available. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Varies by program (LIHEAP, Oregon HEAT, OLGA, OEP, Helping Hands) Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-6492 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONGREGATIONS HELPING PEOPLE UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with past-due electric, trash and recycling, and water bills. Must have a shut-off or disconnect notice. Assistance is limited, dependent on available funding, and available once per household. 1977 Front Street NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 391-4365 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON COORDINATED CARE ORGANIZATION FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (for individuals being discharged from the hospital or who have a set move-in date for a new home and need a temporary place to stay before then) -Electronics such as cell phones, computers, and other communication devices when there are no state programs available in the area -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships or home equipment -Beds and mattresses for children under 21 or for adults with a diagnosis of a back or spine condition -Car seats if there are no other county or programs available in the area All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Visit website to review additional eligibility criteria regarding specific programs and benefits. Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 765-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE WATER AND ELECTRIC BOARD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Electric and water service provider. Fields complaints and calls about utility emergencies. Provides a medical certificate program. May provide assistance or payment plans to customers who are having difficulty paying their utility bills. The Customer Care Program offers a $280 bill credit once per calendar year to eligible customers. 4200 Roosevelt Boulevard Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Electric and water customers: Unrestricted Customer Care Program: EWEB customers that meet income guidelines Hours: Customer Service by phone Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Report power outages and water emergencies 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 685-7000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric, gas, or water bills. Assistance is available only once per lifetime, subject to available funding, and is not guaranteed. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Households must not have received utility related financial assistance from any agency within the past 12 month period. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF LEBANON BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rent assistance, utility assistance, heating fuel assistance, prescription assistance, and clothing. Also offers financial assistance to obtain a valid ID card or driver's license through the Oregon Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV). Assistance is available once per year for each service. 145 W Ash Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 259-3200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEALTH SHARE OF OREGON FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:45pm Email (503) 416-8090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD DUAL HEALTH CREDIT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF OTC plus card or credit help pay for over-the-counter (OTC) healthcare products, healthy foods and household utilities. Covered items and service may include cold care, oral care, pain relief, first aid, incontinence products, grocery items like meat and produce, and utility payments such as electricity, gas, water, telephone, and internet. OTC plus card or credit plan amounts, availability, and items/services covered are dependent on applicant's Coordinated Care Organization (CCO). Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (503) 416-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. Number of times a person can receive assistance: -Active duty member with a rank of E1-E6 may receive 3 total lifetime assists -Currently deployed on Title 10 orders and/are receiving hazardous duty pay may receive 5 total lifetime assists -Documented injury, illness, or wound during post 9/11 service may receive 5 total lifetime assists The number of months of assistance that can be received varies on the type of assistance being requested 17319 San Pedro Avenue Suite 505 San Antonio, TX 78232 Eligibility: Must fit one of three criteria: 1) Wounded, Ill, or Injured Criteria Actively served in the United States military post 9/11 AND suffered a post 9/11 wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty. If discharged, must have Honorable or General (Under Honorable Conditions) discharge within 10 years of discharge. 2) Deployed Criteria A service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS (Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System) eligible/legal dependents AND one of the following criteria's met: -Currently deployed to an overseas location and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -Assigned to a ship that has been at sea for 30+ days and is en route to theater, forward deployed, or otherwise on a mission status, not a training status -On unaccompanied, overseas PCS orders and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -A National Guard member or Reservist on Title 10 orders (not Title 32) who either: Will deploy to an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater in the next 90 days or has returned from an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater within the past 180 days 3) General Criteria -An active duty service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS eligible/legal dependents Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week, leave a message and a coordinator will call back Intake Coordinators: Monday-Friday 7am-3pm Email (210) 659-7756 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOUSING SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with rent and utility assistance (electric, gas, heating fuel, sewer, trash, or water), down payment for a home, or financial counseling, which may include household budgeting, eviction protection, and legal aid. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify. Food boxes are available to households once per month. 29796 SW Town Center Loop E Wilsonville, OR 97070 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines (household income at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level) Hours: Food Pantry: Tuesday 12:30pm-2:30pm and 6pm-7:15pm (last client taken 15 minutes before closing) Office: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday 9am-2pm Email (503) 682-6939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance (electric, gas, and heating fuel) and water when funding is available. 1317 NE Dustin Court McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Renter and owner-occupied households below 60% of Oregon Statewide Median Income Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (503) 687-1480 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY CARE FLEXIBLE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Flexible Services or "flex funds," are non-covered services offered in addition to the covered benefits of the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) through Coordinated Care Organizations (CCOs) to improve care delivery and overall member health and well-being. Eligibility for services is determined by the CCO. Services can include but are not limited to: -Housing support (rent assistance, rental deposit assistance [requires move-in date], utility bills, housing programs for women and children) -Temporary motel stays (For individuals being discharged from the hospital; experiencing an extreme weather event; has a set move-in date for a new home and needs a temporary place to stay before then; or actively fleeing a domestic violence situation.) -Electronics such as cellphones, computers, and other communication devices -Specific educational materials and/or classes -Family and home resources (ex. items for child health and safety, parenting programs, household appliances, climate devices) -Gym memberships and exercise classes/equipment -Health and substance use prevention support All flexible services are determined on a case-by-case basis by the CCO. HRS are NOT intended to address emergency situations. CCOs are not required to offer flexible services. As flexible services are not a covered benefit, a denial cannot be appealed, but an OHP member can submit a complaint. Eligibility: -Must be a current member of the CCO -Member must be unable to receive requested service by any other benefit or means -Item or service requested must help with medical need or life situation -All documentation requested for service must be provided Health-related services are limited, not immediate, not guaranteed or continuously available, and individuals may not qualify.
